name: CYANOTIC HEART DISEASE
definition: PaO2 ≤ 85% ADMIXTURE LESIONS bidirectional shunt with 2 components: two components of
distribution: left-sided SVC: vertical vein of TAPVR

general: skeletal anomalies: Ellis-van Creveld syndrome (truncus / common atrium)
R aortic arch: persistent truncus arteriosus
pulmonary trunk seen: supracardiac TAPVR; DORV; tricuspid atresia; common atrium
ascending aorta with leftward convexity: single ventricle
dilated azygos vein: common atrium + polysplenia + interrupted IVC; TAPVR to
left-sided SVC: vertical vein of TAPVR
prominent L heart border: single ventricle with inverted rudimentary RV; levoposition
age of onset ≤ 2 days: aortic atresia
prominent aorta with L / R aortic arch; inapparent pulmonary trunk
normal-sized heart ← escape mechanism into aorta
